title: lupinus albicaulis var. elatus
intro: Lupinus albicaulis var. elatus, also known as the tall white lupine or a subspecies of the silvery lupine, is a perennial plant that belongs to the legume family and is native to the mountainous regions of western North America. The plant's roots and flowers have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, due to the presence of alkaloids and other compounds. The health benefits of Lupinus albicaulis var. elatus include its potential to treat conditions such as fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as its ability to support the immune system and reduce inflammation. Traditionally, the plant's roots and flowers are prepared as teas, infusions, or tinctures, and can also be dried and used as a powder or in herbal capsules.
